questions.prompt: Which statement appears in this lecture section?
choices: Last week: parallelism within a single GPU This week: parallelism across multiple GPUs In both cases, compute (arithmetic logic units) is far from inputs/outputs (data).
Your parameters (optimizer state + gradients + activations) don't fit on a single GPU.
You want to use more GPUs (more FLOPs) to train faster.
Collective operations are the conceptual primitives used for distributed programming article - These are classic in the parallel programming literature from the 1980s.
questions.answer: Last week: parallelism within a single GPU This week: parallelism across multiple GPUs In both cases, compute (arithmetic logic units) is far from inputs/outputs (data).
